steps.title: Identify the pattern in the table
steps.content:
The outputs are 5, \(\frac{5}{2}\), \(\frac{5}{4}\), \(\frac{5}{8}\). Each output is exactly half the previous, so this is an exponential function with base \(\frac{1}{2}\). The starting value at x = 0 is 5.
$$f(x) = 5 \cdot \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^x = 5 \cdot 2^{-x}$$

steps.title: Test each choice at x = 0 and x = 1
steps.content:
• A — \(f(x) = 5 imes 2^{x+1}\): \(f(0) = 5 imes 2^1 = 10 eq 5\). Fails at x = 0. Wrong.
  • B — \(f(x) = 5 imes 2^x\): \(f(0) = 5\) ✓, but \(f(1) = 10 eq rac{5}{2}\). Fails at x = 1. Wrong.
  • C — \(f(x) = 5 imes 2^{-(x+1)}\): \(f(0) = 5 imes 2^{-1} = rac{5}{2} eq 5\). Fails at x = 0. Wrong.
  • D — \(f(x) = 5 imes 2^{-x}\): \(f(0)=5\) ✓, \(f(1)= rac{5}{2}\) ✓, \(f(2)= rac{5}{4}\) ✓, \(f(3)= rac{5}{8}\) ✓. All values match. That matches choice D — D is correct.
